page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores powered by the 64-bit ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. The architecture allocates exactly one thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio with 8.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 133 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier memory performance, with both small-block and large-block read-only memory bandwidth, as well as large-block write memory bandwidth, placing in the top 10% of tested instances. The Passmark memory score and database operations also rank in the top 10%. Multi-core processing capability is strong, with the Geekbench multi-core score and software compilation benchmarks reaching the top 10%. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Passmark encryption tests scoring in the middle 50%.

This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads, such as Redis in-memory data stores, where it achieves top-tier throughput of 132.72 million SET operations per second. It is also effective for high-throughput static web serving and multi-threaded decompression tasks. While it demonstrates strong text generation speeds for medium and large LLM inference models, its text generation speed for small models is relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25%. The lack of local NVMe storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance utilizing the 64-bit ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 64 dedicated physical cores at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core and 512.0 GB of system memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but features a 133 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show top-tier memory bandwidth and database operations, alongside strong multi-core performance in software compilation and multi-threaded decompression. Conversely, single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation are weaker, falling into average and bottom-tier categories respectively. This instance is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, Redis deployments, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 64 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 512 GB RAM, and 133 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for database and Redis workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.16xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 64 dedicated cores, 512 GB RAM, and 133 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and top-tier memory bandwidth for database and in-memory caching workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARMv9 Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
512.0 GB of system memory delivering top-tier read and write bandwidth
133 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput connectivity
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Optimized for database operations, Redis deployments, and static web serving
